methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
Hidden Level, a Series C defense and security company founded by Jeff Cole and Gary Dominicos, has raised $130M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 12 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising 8 private firms and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Top private peers include Quantum-Systems ($8.0B, Series C), Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), and Saronic ($9.2B, Series D).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement to the $130M raised proxy, implying $221M. Peer-median methodology references the 12-company Defense & Security cohort, which shows a median valuation of $1.8B with a range spanning $300M to $18.0B. The peer-median approach yields $1.8B.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $188M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $998M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in comparable selection within a nascent, high-variance sector where valuation multiples remain unsettled. Stage-progression implies conservatively; peer-median incorporates substantial outliers (Helsing, Saronic). Key risks to the valuation pivot on Hidden Level's relative product maturity, customer concentration, and revenue trajectory versus the peer set.
